entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop (Sorghum bicolor) studied for its phenology in semi-arid tropics

entities.name: PHENOLOGY
entities.description: The study of cyclic and seasonal natural phenomena, specifically the timing of growth stages in sorghum

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The geographical region where the sorghum phenology study was conducted

entities.name: ITERATIVE REGRESSION APPROACH
entities.description: A mathematical method used to derive equations for predicting growth stages and solving non-linear functions

entities.name: GROWTH STAGES
entities.description: The distinct phases of development in the sorghum plant

entities.name: MOISTURE
entities.description: Water availability required for the prediction of growth stages

entities.name: NUTRIENT AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The availability of essential nutrients required for the prediction of growth stages

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: An environmental factor used to predict phenophase duration and growth stages

entities.name: RELATIVE HUMIDITY
entities.description: An environmental factor used to predict phenophase duration and growth stages

entities.name: PHENOPHASE DURATION
entities.description: The length of time a plant spends in a specific phenological phase

entities.name: BASE TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The minimum temperature at which plant growth or development occurs, identified as 10°C

entities.name: POINT OF INFLECTION TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The temperature at which the rate of development changes, identified as 19.6°C

entities.name: GENOTYPES
entities.description: The different genetic variations of sorghum studied for their response to temperature

entities.name: NON-LINEAR FUNCTIONS
entities.description: Mathematical functions that do not form a straight line, solved using the iterative regression approach
